steps.title: Recall the standard form of a circle
steps.content: A circle with center $(h, k)$ and radius $r$ has equation $(x - h)^2 + (y - k)^2 = r^2$. Compare the given equation $(x - 14)^2 + (y - k)^2 = 81$ to this form.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Center $(14, k)$, radius $9$: From $(x - 14)^2$ the $x$-shift is 14, giving $h = 14$. From $(y - k)^2$ the $y$-shift is $k$. And $r^2 = 81$ means $r = 9$. This matches perfectly. ✓
  • Choice B — Center $(k, 14)$, radius $9$: The coordinates of the center are reversed. The $x$-coordinate of the center is 14, not $k$. ✗
  • Choice C — Center $(14, k)$, radius $81$: The center is correct but the radius is wrong. The equation gives $r^2 = 81$, so $r = 9$, not 81. ✗
  • Choice D — Center $(k, 14)$, radius $81$: Both the center coordinates are reversed and the radius is wrong. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
